title: logformatter: link to bats sources on error
body:
We already link to ginkgo sources, now add links to bats.
Ugly, because we need to hardcode containers/podman (git
repo) and test/system (test file path): those can't be
determined from the log results like they can in ginkgo.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Great, may I make another request? It would be cool if a error list could be shown at the end like in the gingo logs.

You mean, more than just the Summary line at the bottom? Something like a list of failed tests? The reason I haven't done that is that it seems like overkill: system test logs are so short that it's super quick & easy to skim via scroll up/down (except when all tests fail, in which case something is seriously wrong, and an error list would not help). The ginkgo logs are impossible for any human to skim, so they truly need a summary.

If I misunderstood your request, could you elaborate?

> You mean, more than just the Summary line at the bottom? Something like a list of failed tests?

Yes, the reason for my request is that you only see the end of the log when you click on the failed run in github. I always have to click on the link just to discover that this a known flake so I press rerun.

Ohhhhh... I get it! Yes, good idea. I've pushed a change that does what I think you mean.

(For benefit of future spelunkers: this part is a change to the *filtering*, not to the *annotated log file*. What @Luap99 refers to is the fact that the log excerpt shown by github shows only the last few lines of the log, and that's of no use whatsoever when the failure happened earlier. With this change, the github excerpt will now include a summary count of failures, with one line showing each failed test name. This could be helpful for quickly distinguishing between known-flake-not-my-problem and uh-oh-this-might-be-my-fault).

No way to test this unless we get a flake, or unless I deliberately introduce a test failure which I'm not that keen on doing atm. But I did test it on my laptop, promise.
